symbols.signature: GET /user/packages/{package_type}/{package_name} -> {..., "visibility": "private"|"public", ...} (read-only field; no PATCH/PUT/mutation exists to set it)
symbols.type_text: verified 2026-07-01 via docs.github.com/en/rest/packages/packages and .../learn-github-packages/configuring-a-packages-access-control-and-visibility: only GET/DELETE/POST-restore are documented for /user/packages/{package_type}/{package_name}; changing visibility is web-UI-only ('Danger Zone' > 'Change visibility'). No GraphQL mutation found either. The coordinator's proposed `gh api --method PATCH .../visibility` endpoint does NOT exist -- corrected to a read + hard-fail verification step (Task 8 Step 4) plus a one-time manual UI fix (Prerequisite 5), not an automatable set-via-API step.

risks.note: WiX Toolset element/attribute names (Custom Action authoring for chained msiexec + CLI post-install steps) were NOT verified against live WiX docs this session — based on general knowledge of WiX v4/v5. The plan-writing and execution agents MUST verify exact WiX syntax (e.g. via context7 or wixtoolset.org docs) before authoring the .wxs project, rather than trusting this bundle for WiX specifics.

The RustDesk config-string value is NOT computable from raw host+key — it must be obtained once by a human (Patrick) manually configuring a RustDesk client and using Settings -> Network -> Export Server Config. This is an operational bootstrap step, not something the implementation can automate or verify in CI beyond consuming the resulting opaque string as a GitHub Actions secret.

All impacted/created file extensions in this change (.yaml, .yml, .wxs) are S1-ungated (limit 0 per scripts/plan-lint.sh _ext_limit default case) — no S1 line-budget constraint applies to this change at all.

GAP FOUND AND FIXED (coordinator-verified, 2026-07-01): the initial plan draft only wired k3d/ (dev) for the downloads service and omitted the prod/ overlay. Without prod/patch-oauth2-proxy-downloads.yaml + prod/ingress.yaml + prod/configmap-domains.yaml + a prod/kustomization.yaml patches: entry, downloads.mentolder.de would only work in local k3d dev — unreachable on the real fleet cluster, defeating REQ-RUSTDESK-CLIENT-003. Confirmed prod/ has no per-brand sub-overlay for docs (no prod-mentolder/ or prod-fleet/mentolder/ override touches it) — only the shared prod/ layer needed new files. Task 4 was inserted to close this gap; downstream tasks renumbered 5-9.

DNS finding (corrects an initial hypothesis that a manual A-record would be needed for downloads.mentolder.de): scripts/fleet-dns-cutover.sh shows the wildcard '*' A-record prefix already round-robins across all three fleet public IPs, and docs.mentolder.de (architecturally identical: Traefik + oauth2-proxy) has no dedicated A-record of its own — it resolves via that same wildcard. downloads.mentolder.de will too. No DNS task/prerequisite was added for it. This is unrelated to rustdesk.mentolder.de's own raw-TCP ID-server hostname, which is a separate, already-solved concern in the archived parent spec (REQ-RUSTDESK-RELAY-005, pinned-node firewall rules).

SECURITY GAP FOUND AND FIXED (coordinator-verified, 2026-07-01): Task 8 pushed ghcr.io/paddione/downloads-content without ever checking its visibility. Confirmed via WebFetch against official GitHub docs: (1) GitHub REST/GraphQL has NO endpoint to SET package visibility -- only GET (read-only field) is documented; changing it is web-UI-only ('Danger Zone'). The coordinator's suggested `gh api PATCH .../visibility` call does not exist and would 404 if implemented as suggested -- do not add it verbatim. (2) GitHub docs state personal-account packages default to private on first publish, BUT a package auto-linked to its source repo (e.g. via an OCI image-source LABEL, or an Actions-published package connected to the repo) can instead inherit that repo's visibility -- public here, since this repo is public. This is the concrete mechanism that could make downloads-content public despite the general private-by-default rule, and is exactly the bypass REQ-RUSTDESK-CLIENT-003 exists to prevent (SSO gate skipped via direct `docker pull` + layer extraction of the baked password). Fix applied: Task 8 Step 4 adds a read + hard-fail verification (`gh api /user/packages/container/downloads-content --jq '.visibility'`, exit 1 if not 'private') that runs on EVERY workflow run (not just first), Prerequisite 5 documents the one-time manual UI check/fix (no automation possible), and the Dockerfile deliberately omits the org.opencontainers.image.source LABEL as a preventive (not sufficient on its own) measure.

UNVERIFIED this session: whether a classic PAT's `write:packages` scope (already granted to GH_PAT for docker/login-action push access) implicitly also grants `read:packages` (needed for the new Task 8 Step 4 `gh api GET /user/packages/container/...` call), or whether both scopes must be requested independently. Official docs list them as separate scopes without stating the hierarchy. If Step 4 fails with an HTTP 403 (as opposed to a 'visibility != private' failure), add the `read:packages` scope to the existing GH_PAT.
